goal: Add upstream auth-error detection to server.py: a _check_upstream_auth_error() helper, calls in /ask and /plan, HTTPException propagation guard, and full unit tests for all new behaviour.

tasks.name: Add _check_upstream_auth_error helper
tasks.action: Define a module-level helper function `_check_upstream_auth_error(text: str) -> None` in server.py. The function searches `text` for the substrings `'OAuth token has expired'` and `'authentication_error'`. If either marker is found it raises `HTTPException(status_code=502, detail='Upstream API authentication failure — please refresh your ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.')`. If neither marker is found it returns None.
tasks.verify: Run `python -c "from server import _check_upstream_auth_error; _check_upstream_auth_error('authentication_error')"` and confirm it raises HTTPException(502). Run the same import with a benign string and confirm no exception is raised.
tasks.done: Function `_check_upstream_auth_error` exists in server.py, raises HTTPException 502 on auth markers, and returns None otherwise.

tasks.name: Call helper in /ask and /plan on subprocess failure
tasks.action: In the /ask endpoint handler, after the `subprocess.run(...)` call: (a) if `result.returncode != 0`, call `_check_upstream_auth_error(result.stderr)` before any other error handling; (b) after parsing the JSON response, if the parsed result indicates a JSON-level error (e.g. `result_text` contains an error field), call `_check_upstream_auth_error(result_text)`. Apply the identical two call-sites in the /plan endpoint handler.
tasks.verify: Code-review server.py: both /ask and /plan each contain exactly two calls to `_check_upstream_auth_error` — one guarding `result.returncode != 0` / `result.stderr`, one guarding the parsed JSON error path.
tasks.done: _check_upstream_auth_error is called in both endpoints at both the stderr (non-zero exit) and parsed-JSON-error paths.

tasks.name: Guard HTTPException propagation in /ask and /plan
tasks.action: In both the /ask and /plan endpoint handlers, locate the generic `except Exception as e:` block. Immediately before it, insert `except HTTPException: raise` so that HTTPException (including the 502 raised by _check_upstream_auth_error) is re-raised rather than caught and swallowed by the generic handler.
tasks.verify: Code-review server.py: both /ask and /plan contain `except HTTPException: raise` placed before `except Exception`.
tasks.done: Both endpoints have `except HTTPException: raise` directly above their generic `except Exception` block.

tasks.name: Unit tests for _check_upstream_auth_error
tasks.action: In tests/test_server.py, add a test class (or group of test functions) for `_check_upstream_auth_error`: (1) test_auth_error_oauth_marker — passes a string containing `'OAuth token has expired'`, asserts HTTPException is raised with status_code 502; (2) test_auth_error_authentication_error_marker — passes a string containing `'authentication_error'`, asserts HTTPException 502; (3) test_auth_error_no_marker — passes a benign string, asserts return value is None and no exception is raised.
tasks.verify: Run `pytest tests/test_server.py -k 'auth_error' -v` and confirm all three new tests pass.
tasks.done: Three tests for _check_upstream_auth_error exist in tests/test_server.py and all pass.

tasks.name: Integration-style unit tests for /ask and /plan with auth error
tasks.action: In tests/test_server.py, add two tests using the FastAPI TestClient and `unittest.mock.patch('subprocess.run')`: (1) test_ask_returns_502_on_oauth_stderr — mock subprocess.run to return a CompletedProcess with returncode=1 and stderr containing `'OAuth token has expired'`; POST to /ask with a valid bearer token and assert the response status code is 502; (2) test_plan_returns_502_on_oauth_stderr — identical setup but POST to /plan. Both tests must use whatever auth header/token the existing tests use.
tasks.verify: Run `pytest tests/test_server.py -k '502_on_oauth' -v` and confirm both new tests pass.
tasks.done: Two endpoint tests (one for /ask, one for /plan) exist in tests/test_server.py, mock subprocess stderr with the OAuth marker, and both assert HTTP 502 in the response.
